claims: A program can accept the lengths of four sides of a polygon as input.
A program can calculate and display the perimeter of a polygon given the lengths of its four sides.
An algorithm can determine the number of years until a father's age is double his son's age.
An algorithm can output the real solutions of a second-order polynomial equation of the form ax² + bx + c = 0, given parameters a, b, and c.
A second-order polynomial equation can have one or two real solutions.
